group_context: Questions 27-28 refer to the following information. Consider the following sort method. This method correctly sorts the elements of array data into increasing order. The code shown consists of 18 lines Line 1: public static void sort ( int [ ] data ) Lin e 2: { Line 3: for ( int j = zero ; j less t han da ta dot length - 1 ; j + + ) Line 4: { Line 5 : int m = j ; Line 6: for ( int k = j + 1 ; k less than data dot length ; k + + ) Line 7: { Line 8: if ( data [ k ] less than data [ m ] ) / * Compare values * / Line 9: { Line 10: m = k ; Line 11: } Line 12: } Line 13: int temp = data [ m ] ; / * Assign to temp * / Line 14: data [ m ] = data [ j ] ; Line 15: data [ j ] = temp ; Line 16: / * End of outer loop * / Line 17: } Line 18: }

group_context: Questions 27-28 refer to the following information. Consider the following sort method. This method correctly sorts the elements of array data into increasing order. The code shown consists of 18 lines Line 1: public static void sort ( int [ ] data ) Lin e 2: { Line 3: for ( int j = zero ; j less t han da ta dot length - 1 ; j + + ) Line 4: { Line 5 : int m = j ; Line 6: for ( int k = j + 1 ; k less than data dot length ; k + + ) Line 7: { Line 8: if ( data [ k ] less than data [ m ] ) / * Compare values * / Line 9: { Line 10: m = k ; Line 11: } Line 12: } Line 13: int temp = data [ m ] ; / * Assign to temp * / Line 14: data [ m ] = data [ j ] ; Line 15: data [ j ] = temp ; Line 16: / * End of outer loop * / Line 17: } Line 18: }

group_context: Questions 36-37 refer to the following information. Consider the following binary Search, all one word and capital S – henceforth referred to as binary Search. method. The method correctly performs a binary search. The code shown consists of 23 lines Line 1: / * * Precondition colon data is sorted i n increasing orde r * / Lin e 2: public static int bina ry Search ( int [ ] data comma int target ) Line 3: { Line 4: int start = zero ; Line 5: int end = data dot length - 1 ; Line 6: while ( start less than or equal to end ) Line 7: { Line 8: int mid = ( start + end ) / 2 ; / * Calculate midpoint * / Line 9: if ( target less than data [ mid ] ) Line 10: { Line 11: end = mid - 1 ; Line 12: } Line 13: else if ( target greater than data [ mid ] ) Line 14: { Line 15: start = mid + 1 ; Line 16: } Line 17: else Line 18: { Line 19: return mid, ; Line 20: } Line 21: } Line 22: return negative 1 ; Line 23: }

The method correctly performs a binary search. The code shown consists of 23 lines Line 1: / * * Precondition colon data is sorted i n increasing orde r * / Lin e 2: public static int bina ry Search ( int [ ] data comma int target ) Line 3: { Line 4: int start = zero ; Line 5: int end = data dot length - 1 ; Line 6: while ( start less than or equal to end ) Line 7: { Line 8: int mid = ( start + end ) / 2 ; / * Calculate midpoint * / Line 9: if ( target less than data [ mid ] ) Line 10: { Line 11: end = mid - 1 ; Line 12: } Line 13: else if ( target greater than data [ mid ] ) Line 14: { Line 15: start = mid + 1 ; Line 16: } Line 17: else Line 18: { Line 19: return mid, ; Line 20: } Line 21: } Line 22: return negative 1 ; Line 23: }

group_context: Questions 10-11 refer to the following information. Consider the following instance variable and methods. You may assume that data has been initialized with length > 0. The methods are intended to return the index of an array element equal to target, or -1 if no such element exists. private int[] data; public int seqSearchRec(int target) { return seqSearchRecHelper(target, data.length - 1); } private int seqSearchRecHelper(int target, int last) { // Line 1 if (data[last] == target) return last; else return seqSearchRecHelper(target, last - 1); }

group_context: Questions 11–12 refer to the following information. Consider the following instance variable nums and method findLongest with line numbers added for reference. Method findLongest is intended to find the longest consecutive block of the value target occurring in the array nums; however, findLongest does not work as intended. For example, if the array nums contains the values [7, 10, 10, 15, 15, 15, 15, 10, 10, 10, 15, 10, 10], the call findLongest(10) should return 3, the length of the longest consecutive block of 10s. 28
